# Prompt Template: Brand Identity Guide

Use after you have a visual prototype. Paste into your design tool or AI with screenshots of what you've built.

---

```
I want you to help me create a brand style guide for this product based on what we've built so far. Think like a designer handing off a project — capture everything another developer or tool would need to keep the product visually consistent.

Structure the document as follows:

1. BRAND OVERVIEW
- What is the personality of this product? (professional, playful, bold, minimal, etc.)
- What impression should a user get within the first 5 seconds?
- What existing brands or products is this most visually similar to?

2. COLOR PALETTE
- Primary, secondary, and accent colors (with hex values)
- Background and surface colors
- Semantic colors (success/green, warning/yellow, error/red, info/blue)
- Text colors (primary, secondary, muted)
- How and where each color is used

3. TYPOGRAPHY
- Font families (headings, body, monospace/code if applicable)
- Font sizes and weights for each level (h1-h4, body, caption, label)
- Line heights and letter spacing
- Any Google Fonts or system font stacks being used

4. SPACING & LAYOUT
- Base spacing unit (e.g., 4px grid)
- Common padding/margin patterns
- Card, container, and section spacing conventions
- Grid or layout system being used

5. COMPONENT PATTERNS
- What do cards, buttons, inputs, modals, and alerts look like?
- Border radius conventions
- Shadow/elevation patterns
- Hover and active states
- Status indicators (how we communicate red/yellow/green, progress, etc.)

6. ICONOGRAPHY & IMAGERY
- Icon style (outlined, filled, duotone) and source (Lucide, Heroicons, etc.)
- Any illustration or image style conventions

7. DO's AND DON'Ts
- 3-5 concrete guidelines for maintaining visual consistency
- Common mistakes to avoid

We will be working toward a brand.md file — a reference document we'll give to any AI tool or developer working on this project to keep it visually consistent.
```
